quick_skim.title: Pupil-DLC: an open-source deep learning pipeline for scalable, marker-less tracking of pupil dynamics across conscious and unconscious states
quick_skim.abstract: Background: Pupil diameter is a non-invasive biomarker of brain state, correlating with arousal, attention, cognitive processing, and consciousness. However, existing pupillometry software often lacks scalability and robustness across diverse experimental conditions and species. New method: We introduce Pupil-DLC, an open-source, offline, DeepLabCut-based pipeline for scalable, marker-less pupil tracking, primarily designed for mice. Trained on 21,750 manually annotated frames from over 140 videos of head-fixed mice spanning wakefulness and drug-induced states, including psychedelics and anesthesia, the dataset was deliberately selected to maximize pupil size variability and model generalization. Pupil-DLC implements a dual-model architecture: a General Model (GM) for high-throughput analysis and an Individual Model (IM) for session-specific optimization. Results: Pupil-DLC captures pupil dynamics across awake, psychedelic, and anesthetized conditions with high agreement with ground truth and equal tracking fidelity during active locomotion and quiet rest. Confidence metrics aligned with human frame quality assessments, enabling principled tuning of accuracy-retention trade-offs. As a secondary demonstration, Pupil-DLC extends to unseen human videos across diverse conditions and frame rates, including daylight and smartphone recordings, without retraining. Comparison with existing methods: Pupil-DLC outperforms existing automated methods in accuracy and frame retention while maintaining computational efficiency comparable to real-time tools. These improvements stem from a learned keypoint-based representation robust to pupil shape variability, occlusions, reflections, and imaging artifacts. The GM/IM framework supports a tiered strategy balancing throughput and precision. Conclusions: Pupil-DLC provides a reproducible, adaptable platform for quantifying pupil-linked brain state dynamics across experimental paradigms and species, bridging basic mouse neuroscience and translational human applications.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Pupil diameter tracking pipeline using deep learning for scalable marker-less pupillometry
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于深度学习的瞳孔直径跟踪管道，用于可扩展的无标记瞳孔测量
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于深度学习的瞳孔直径跟踪管道，用于可扩展的无标记瞳孔测量
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于深度学习的瞳孔直径跟踪管道，用于可扩展的无标记瞳孔测量
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Pupil-DLC provides an open-source deep learning pipeline for marker-less pupil tracking, enabling robust pupillometry across diverse experimental conditions; its method could be transferred to human display studies for pupil-based brightness perception modeling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有瞳孔测量软件在跨实验条件和物种中缺乏可扩展性和鲁棒性的问题，本文提出Pupil-DLC，一种基于DeepLabCut的开源无标记瞳孔跟踪管道。该管道在包含清醒和药物诱导状态的小鼠视频大数据集上训练，能够可靠提取瞳孔直径。该工具为跨意识状态的瞳孔动力学研究提供了可扩展平台，其方法可迁移到人类显示研究中用于基于瞳孔的亮度感知建模。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有瞳孔测量软件在跨实验条件和物种中缺乏可扩展性和鲁棒性的问题，本文提出Pupil-DLC，一种基于DeepLabCut的开源无标记瞳孔跟踪管道。该管道在包含清醒和药物诱导状态的小鼠视频大数据集上训练，能够可靠提取瞳孔直径。该工具为跨意识状态的瞳孔动力学研究提供了可扩展平台，其方法可迁移到人类显示研究中用于基于瞳孔的亮度感知建模。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Find literature on perceived display brightness models using pupil diameter, retinal imaging, ambient illumination, and visual comfort for AR, OLED, or mobile displays.
